what: Bayesian Mixed Graphical model for Inference on Mixed Data
title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical model for Inference on Mixed Data
text: BMGM (Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model) R package Bayesian inference mixed graphical models, allowing estimation conditional dependencies continuous, discrete, categorical, zero-inflated count data. package employs MCMC sampling spike--slab priors structure learning can handle missing data inference.

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical model for Inference on Mixed Data
text: install package GitHub, run:

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical model for Inference on Mixed Data
code: library(BMGM)  set.seed(123) X <- matrix(rnorm(50), 10, 5) type <- rep("c", 5)  # All continuous variables  fit <- bmgm(X, type, nburn = 500, nsample = 1000) fit$adj_G

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical model for Inference on Mixed Data
text: bmgm(): Fit Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model find_lambda(): Optimize transformation parameter (Arkaprava & Dunson, 2020) F_transformation(): Apply transformation mixed data

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical model for Inference on Mixed Data
text: Released MIT License. Contributions welcome!

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model — bmgm
text: Implements Bayesian approach inference mixed graphical models. Estimates conditional independencies variables mixed types (continuous, discrete, categorical, zero-inflated count data) using MCMC algorithm posterior inference.

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model — bmgm
code: bmgm(   X,   type,   nburn = 1000,   nsample = 1000,   theta_priors,   v_0 = 0.05,   v_1 = 1,   pi_beta,   seed,   context_spec = T,   bfdr = 0.05,   cont = FALSE,   ... )

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model — bmgm
text: X numeric matrix data frame containing data (mixed type variables). type character vector indicating type variable. Options: "c" (continuous), "d" (discrete), "z" (zero-inflated count), "m" (categorical). nburn Number burn-iterations MCMC. Default 1000. nsample Number MCMC samples collect burn-. Default 1000. theta_priors List priors node parameters. v_0 Prior variance spike spike--slab. Default 0.05. v_1 Prior variance slab spike--slab. Default 1. pi_beta Prior probability edge inclusion. Default `2/(p-1)`. seed Random seed reproducibility. context_spec Logical, whether compute context-specific adjacency matrix. Default TRUE. bfdr Bayesian False Discovery Rate threshold edge selection. Default 0.05. cont Logical, whether transform continuous variables. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments.

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model — bmgm
text: list containing: post_Beta Posterior samples precision matrix. post_theta Posterior samples node parameters. post_Z Posterior samples edge indicators. adj_Beta Estimated adjacency matrix dependencies. adj_G Adjacency matrix edge selection. lambda Estimated transformation parameter.

title: Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model — bmgm
code: # Simulated example data set.seed(123) X1 <- rnorm(100) X2 <- rpois(100, lambda = 5) X3 <- rnorm(100, mean = X2) X4 <- rpois(100, lambda = exp(X1)) X <- cbind(X1,X2,X3,X4) type <- c("c", "d", "c", "d")  # Fit Bayesian Mixed Graphical Model fit <- bmgm(X, type, nburn = 500, nsample = 1000)  # Print adjacency matrix print(fit$adj_G) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] #> [1,]    0    0    1    1 #> [2,]    0    0    1    0 #> [3,]    1    1    0    1 #> [4,]    1    0    1    0

what: Convert Category Graph to General Graph — categories_graph
title: Convert Category Graph to General Graph — categories_graph
text: function converts graph estimated categorical variables general adjacency matrix representation.

title: Convert Category Graph to General Graph — categories_graph
code: categories_graph(q, p, var_names, esti_Z, esti_Beta, categories)

title: Convert Category Graph to General Graph — categories_graph
text: q Number transformed variables p Number original variables var_names Vector original variable names esti_Z Adjacency matrix context-specific inference esti_Beta Estimated beta coefficients graph edges categories Vector indicating number categories variable

title: Convert Category Graph to General Graph — categories_graph
text: list : Adj_Beta Estimated adjacency matrix Beta Adj_Z Estimated adjacency matrix Z

what: Transformation Function for Mixed Graphical Models — F_transformation
title: Transformation Function for Mixed Graphical Models — F_transformation
text: Performs transformation input data based type. Used continuous, discrete, zero-inflated data Mixed Graphical Models.

title: Transformation Function for Mixed Graphical Models — F_transformation
Transformation Function for Mixed Graphical Models — F_transformation
text: X numeric matrix vector. type character vector specifying type variable. Options: "c" (continuous), "d" (discrete), "z" (zero-inflated), "m" (categorical). parameter Numeric parameter used transformation. cont Logical. TRUE, continuous variables transformed. Default FALSE.

title: Transformation Function for Mixed Graphical Models — F_transformation
text: matrix transformed data.

what: Gibbs Sampler for Bayesian Mixed Graphical Models — sampler_bmgm
title: Gibbs Sampler for Bayesian Mixed Graphical Models — sampler_bmgm
text: Implements Gibbs sampler Bayesian inference Mixed Graphical Models.

title: Gibbs Sampler for Bayesian Mixed Graphical Models — sampler_bmgm
code: sampler_bmgm(   n = 1,   Beta,   theta,   type,   categories,   lambda,   M = 1000,   X_new,   variables,   std )

title: Gibbs Sampler for Bayesian Mixed Graphical Models — sampler_bmgm
text: n Number samples generate. Beta Precision matrix graphical model. theta list parameters node. type Character vector indicating variable types. categories Vector indicating number categories (categorical variables). lambda Transformation parameter. M Number Gibbs sampler iterations. Default 1000. X_new Vector known values imputation. Default `c(0,0,0)`. variables Indicator vector specifying variables update. std Standard deviation used transformation.

title: Gibbs Sampler for Bayesian Mixed Graphical Models — sampler_bmgm
text: matrix containing sampled data.

title: Gibbs Sampler for Bayesian Mixed Graphical Models — sampler_bmgm
code: # Simulate example data set.seed(123) Beta <- diag(4)  # Identity precision matrix theta <- list(c(0,1), c(0,1), c(0.5,1), c(0.5,1)) type <- c("c", "c", "d", "d") categories <- c(1,1,1,1) lambda <- 1  # Run the Gibbs sampler samples <- sampler_bmgm(n = 10, Beta = Beta, theta = theta, type = type,                         categories = categories, lambda = lambda, M = 500)  # View first few samples head(samples) #>            [,1]       [,2] [,3] [,4] #> [1,]  0.2328947 -0.9503090    0    1 #> [2,] -0.2665211 -1.2780180    0    1 #> [3,]  1.0395558  0.4466522    1    0 #> [4,]  1.4281302 -0.8506573    0    0 #> [5,]  0.8696172 -1.2896855    1    0 #> [6,]  0.1576859 -0.4397891    0    1
